name: effective-manager
description: Codex skills for the tactical day-to-day mechanics of engineering management: first-week snapshot, manager toolkit, 1-1s, performance reviews, hiring, motivation, stress and pressure, information and politics, self-care, team housekeeping, and career vision. Derived from James Stanier's Become an Effective Software Engineering Manager.

interface.shortDescription: Tactical skills for the day-to-day mechanics of engineering management.
interface.longDescription: A Codex plugin that bundles the tactical, day-to-day skills of being an effective engineering manager: the first-week snapshot (Venn-diagram exercise), the manager toolkit (calendar, to-do, email, capture), Stanier-style 1-1s with contracting, performance reviews on a 6-month cadence, hiring funnels and interview discipline, motivation and fit, stress and pressure handling, information sharing and politics, self-care, team housekeeping (Conway's Law, ADRs, five whys, health checks, DRIs), and the 10-year career vision exercise. Skills are grounded in James Stanier's Become an Effective Software Engineering Manager.
defaultPrompt: Use effective-manager skills to help me lead my team this week.
Use first-week-snapshot to structure my first week as the new manager of this team.
Use manager-toolkit to audit how I spend my time and clean up my inputs.
Use one-to-ones-stanier to run a contracting session with my new direct report.
Use one-to-ones-stanier to prep this week's 1-1 with this report.
Use motivation-and-fit to figure out what would re-engage this report.
Use performance-reviews-stanier to set up the review tracker for the next cycle.
Use hiring-stanier to draft a JD and design the funnel for this role.
Use stress-and-pressure to plan what to cut as this deadline approaches.
Use information-and-politics to decide what I can share with the team about this.
Use letting-go-and-self-care to help me stop thinking about work in the evening.
Use team-housekeeping to run a five whys on last week's incident.
Use career-vision-exercise to deliver a 10-year career conversation to this senior engineer.
